candidate_summary: K-shide-cluster boundary marker tokens at L_int intervals, left-twist asymmetric leftward earlier-context attention at boundaries, ward-evil token-purification gate G_purify with impurity-score subspace V_purify routing, Futo-tama demarcation prefix-token preserves against context bleed-back, gradient-taper attention magnitude α(p) thicker at boundary positions.

amaterasu futo-tama cave demarcation kami spirit
ward-evil purification torii shrine boundary marker
left-twist counter-clockwise gradient taper rice straw hemp
K-shide-cluster boundary marker tokens every L_int interval inserted in context
left-twist input-direction asymmetry leftward earlier-context attention favored at boundaries
ward-evil token-purification gate G_purify impurity-score subspace V_purify routing
Futo-tama demarcation prefix-token preserves against context bleed-back + gradient-taper attention magnitude thicker at boundaries

results.name: Gated Attention NeurIPS 2025 Non-Linearity Sparsity Sink-Free
results.rationale: Gated attention mechanism shares gating concept with G_purify but lacks boundary markers, left-twist asymmetry, shide-cluster tokens, or purification subspace routing.

results.name: Gated Attention OpenReview NeurIPS 2025 Oral
results.rationale: Same as #1, gated attention only. Does not implement K-shide-cluster boundary tokens, leftward directional asymmetry, or impurity-score subspace V_purify.

results.name: When Attention Sink Emerges ICLR 2025
results.rationale: Attention sink emergence analysis differs from shimenawa boundary-gate purification mechanism. No boundary-marker interval tokens, no purification gate, no left-twist directional asymmetry.

results.name: Value-State Gated Attention Extreme-Token
results.rationale: Value-state gating against extreme tokens has thematic parallel to purification-gating against impure context, but lacks K-shide-cluster interval boundary insertion, leftward asymmetry, and Futo-tama prefix demarcation.

results.name: Long-Context Generalization ICLR 2026
results.rationale: Long-context generalization is generic and does not match boundary-marker shimenawa rope tokens, purification subspace routing, or left-twist directional input asymmetry.

results.name: Attention Sinks Diffusion LMs
results.rationale: Attention sinks in diffusion LMs unrelated to K-shide-cluster boundary tokens at L_int intervals, ward-evil purification gating, or gradient-taper boundary attention magnitude.

results.name: Model Knows Tokens Matter Noise Gating
results.rationale: Noise gating shares purification-gating spirit (removing impure/noisy tokens) but does not implement shide-cluster boundary markers, left-twist leftward asymmetry, or Futo-tama prefix preservation.

results.name: SinkTrack Attention Sink BOS Anchor
results.rationale: BOS-anchor sink tracking parallels Futo-tama prefix-token concept loosely, but lacks K-shide-cluster periodic boundary insertion, ward-evil purification subspace V_purify, leftward directional asymmetry, and gradient-taper α(p) at boundaries.

decision_rationale: None of the 8 results meet any threshold. Closest parallels (gated attention, BOS-anchor sinks, noise gating) only weakly match individual aspects. NOTE: primary scored 8 hits at sem 0.74-0.92 / func 0.74-0.92 on Gated-Attention + SinkTrack + Value-State adjacencies; verdict-level disagreement flagged (Pattern E).
